title: Tactics: Trapping a Piece
summary: Learn to trap opponent's pieces and win material.
description: Trapping a piece is a tactical theme where you force an enemy piece into a position where it has no escape squares or cannot be defended. The classic example is luring a bishop or knight forward where it can be trapped by pawns. Essential for all levels.
objectives: Recognize when a piece can be lured into a trap
Understand how to block escape routes
plans: Look for pieces that have advanced too far
Block those squares with pawns or pieces
commonMistakes: Don't try to trap a piece that has a clear escape
Make sure you can afford to spend moves trapping
Sometimes the trapped piece can defend itself
completionSummary: You've learned to identify and execute piece trapping tactics.

steps.title: Control the center
steps.hint: Push the king pawn two squares.

steps.title: Recapture
steps.hint: Capture on d4 with the c-pawn.

steps.title: Black develops
steps.instruction: Black moves the bishop to b6.

steps.title: Push e5
steps.instruction: Play e5! This attacks the knight.

steps.title: Knight to g4
steps.instruction: Black moves the knight to g4.
steps.coaching: The knight attacks h2. But let's trap it!

steps.title: Set the trap
steps.instruction: Play d5! This lures the knight forward.

steps.title: Knight captures
steps.coaching: The knight takes the pawn. Now let's trap it!

steps.title: Attack the knight
steps.instruction: Move Nf4! This attacks the knight and cuts off escape!

steps.title: Knight retreats
steps.coaching: The knight is trapped! We've won a pawn through trapping.
